audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:imperative + anders + consequence]] and [[grammar:euro amount + cent amount]]. Use [[grammar:anders]] for otherwise. Say the euros first and the cents after them.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:imperative + anders + consequence]]: [[grammar:Anders]] links an implicit negative condition to its consequence and keeps main-clause V2 order.; [[grammar:euro amount + cent amount]]: Dutch price expressions may be spoken as euro and cent units; decimal comma notation corresponds to the same value.

audienceSections.normal:
#### imperative + anders + consequence

Use [[grammar:anders]] for otherwise. Example: [[grammar:Neem ook een tas mee, anders valt het brood.]]

#### euro amount + cent amount

Say the euros first and the cents after them. Example: [[grammar:De appels kosten drie euro en vijftig cent.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### imperative + anders + consequence

[[grammar:Anders]] links an implicit negative condition to its consequence and keeps main-clause V2 order. Literal example: [[grammar:Neem ook een tas mee, anders valt het brood.]]

#### euro amount + cent amount

Dutch price expressions may be spoken as euro and cent units; decimal comma notation corresponds to the same value. Literal example: [[grammar:De appels kosten drie euro en vijftig cent.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Anders]] introduces an undesirable alternative consequence. Prices can be spoken with separate [[grammar:euro]] and [[grammar:cent]] units.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Anders]] introduces an undesirable alternative consequence. Prices can be spoken with separate [[grammar:euro]] and [[grammar:cent]] units. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:De appels kosten drie euro en vijftig cent.]] — The apples cost three euros and fifty cents.
- [[grammar:Dat is een goede prijs.]] — That is a good price.
- [[grammar:De komkommers kosten twee euro en twintig cent.]] — The cucumbers cost two euros and twenty cents.
- [[grammar:Is de kaas goedkoop vandaag?]] — Is the cheese cheap today?
- [[grammar:Ja, de prijs is vier euro en tien cent.]] — Yes, the price is four euros and ten cents.
- [[grammar:Ik betaal contant met tien euro.]] — I will pay ten euros in cash.
- [[grammar:Dan krijg je twintig cent wisselgeld.]] — Then you receive twenty cents in change.
- [[grammar:Controleer het bedrag, anders klopt de kassa niet.]] — Check the amount, otherwise the register will not balance.
- [[grammar:Precies. Neem ook een tas mee, anders valt het brood.]] — Exactly. Take a bag too, otherwise the bread will fall.
- [[grammar:Hoeveel kost alles samen?]] — How much does everything cost together?
- [[grammar:De totale prijs is negen euro en tachtig cent.]] — The total price is nine euros and eighty cents.
- [[grammar:Goed, het wisselgeld klopt precies.]] — Good, the change is exactly right.
